docs.text: Welcome to SceneShift SceneShift is a terminal-first process optimizer built with Go and Bubble Tea . It is designed for gamers and 3D artists who need every bit of performance. SceneShift allows you to kill or suspend background applications to free up CPU and RAM before launching a heavy task—clean, fast, and zero-bloat. Think of it as switching into a new "performance scene" for your machine. Key Features 🚀 Smart Process Control: Kill or suspend multiple apps with a single toggle. ⏸️ Suspend Mode: Pause background apps (like Spotify or Chrome) to free up resources without closing them completely. 📊 Snapshot RAM: Get real-time feedback on exactly how much memory (MB/GB) was reclaimed. 🎨 Modular Theming: Visual settings are separated into theme.yaml so you can swap styles easily. 📁 Presets: Switch between "Gaming", "Rendering", or custom profiles instantly. 🛡️ System Integration: Automatically handles Admin elevation for managing protected processes.
docs.title: Home

docs.title: Key Features

docs.text: Configuration SceneShift uses two YAML files to keep your logic and visuals separate. These are generated automatically on the first run. 1. config.yaml This file controls logic : keybindings, presets, and the applications you want to manage. hotkeys: up: - up - k down: - down - j toggle: - ' ' select_all: - a deselect_all: - x kill_mode: - K restore_mode: - R quit: - q - ctrl+c help: - '?' presets: [] apps: [] 2. theme.yaml This file controls visuals. You can change colors here without affecting functionality. Default Theme: Rose Pine Moon name: Rose Pine Moon base: '#232136' surface: '#2a273f' text: '#e0def4' highlight: '#3e8fb0' select: '#c4a7e7' kill: '#eb6f92' restore: '#9ccfd8' warn: '#f6c177'
docs.title: Configuration

docs.text: Installation SceneShift is built for Windows 10 and 11 . Method 1: Scoop (Recommended) The easiest way to install and keep SceneShift updated is using the Scoop package manager. Open PowerShell and run: # 1. Add the bucket scoop bucket add tandukuda https://github.com/tandukuda/scoop-bucket # 2. Install SceneShift scoop install sceneshift Method 2: Manual Installation If you prefer not to use a package manager, you can run the standalone binary. Download: Get the latest release from the Releases Page . Organize: Create a new folder (e.g., Documents/SceneShift ) and move SceneShift.exe inside. !!! warning "Important" Do not run the app directly from your Desktop or Downloads! On the first run, SceneShift automatically generates config.yaml and theme.yaml . Keeping it in a separate folder ensures your files stay organized. Run: Double-click SceneShift.exe . Note: Windows will request Administrator access to manage processes. Shortcut: Right-click SceneShift.exe → Send to → Desktop (create shortcut) for quick access later. Method 3: Build From Source If you prefer to build it yourself, you will need Go 1.21+ installed. # 1. Clone the repo git clone https://github.com/tandukuda/SceneShift.git cd SceneShift # 2. Install resource tool (for icon embedding) go install github.com/akavel/rsrc@latest # 3. Build with assets rsrc -manifest sceneshift.manifest -ico assets/icon.ico -o sceneshift.syso go build -ldflags "-s -w" -o SceneShift.exe
docs.title: Getting Started

docs.text: Safety & Disclaimer !!! warning "System Stability Warning" SceneShift interacts directly with system processes. While the application is built with safety in mind, terminating essential system applications or drivers may cause system instability, crashes, or data loss. - Always save your work in other applications before using Kill Mode. - Avoid terminating processes if you are unsure of what they do. - Use the "Suspend" feature if you are hesitant about killing a process entirely. License SceneShift is distributed under the MIT License.
docs.title: Safety & Disclaimer

While the application is built with safety in mind, terminating essential system applications or drivers may cause system instability, crashes, or data loss. - Always save your work in other applications before using Kill Mode. - Avoid terminating processes if you are unsure of what they do. - Use the "Suspend" feature if you are hesitant about killing a process entirely.

docs.text: Troubleshooting "Access Denied" Errors SceneShift interacts with system processes, which requires high-level permissions. * Solution: Ensure you grant the Administrator permission request when launching the application. If you clicked "No", restart the app. Config Files Not Appearing If config.yaml or theme.yaml are not generated: * Solution: Check if you are running the .exe from a protected folder (like Program Files ). Move the executable to a user folder like Documents/SceneShift or your Desktop. App Not Restarting (Restore Mode) If pressing R does not reopen an app: * Solution: The app might be missing its exec_path . Use the Edit ( e ) function in the UI to manually add the file path to the executable (e.g., C:\Program Files\Adobe\Photoshop.exe ).
docs.title: Troubleshooting

docs.title: App Not Restarting (Restore Mode)

docs.text: Usage Guide SceneShift is keyboard-centric. You can navigate the interface entirely without a mouse. Navigation & Controls Action Input Description Navigate ↑ ↓ or k j Scroll through the app list. Toggle Space Select/Deselect the highlighted app. Select All a Select all visible apps. Deselect All x Clear all current selections. Find/Search Ctrl + f Search running apps to add them quickly. Help ? Toggle the help menu overlay. Modes & Actions Action Input Description Kill Mode K Terminate selected apps. Displays "RAM Reclaimed" stats. Restore Mode R Relaunch apps (requires exec_path to be set). Apply Preset 1 - 9 Instantly apply a saved preset (e.g., Gaming, Rendering). Management & Editors Action Input Description New App n Add a new app to the tracking list. Edit App e Edit the currently selected app configuration. Delete App d Remove the selected app. Theme Selector t Switch or edit themes live. Preset Editor p Open the editor to add or modify presets.
docs.title: Usage
